content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 16, 2005

Councilman Mike Preston
City of San Marcos
1 Civic Center Drive
San Marcos, CA 92069-2918

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-015

Dear Mr. Preston:

This letter is in response to your letter of January 30, 2005, requesting advice regarding procedures for waiving a stipend in order to avoid a conflict of interest under your local ordinance. The conflict of interest is based on the council member's receipt of a contribution. The Commission does not provide advice when the request does not present a question under the Act¹ or the question presented is purely hypothetical. (Gov. Code section 83114; regulation 18329, subdivisions (b)(8)(D) and (c)(4)(D) and (F), copy enclosed.)

Because the facts do not present a question under the Act and the question presented was purely hypothetical we declined to provide assistance. You have posed an additional three questions seeking advice as to how the waiver may be implemented and enforced, based on a hypothetical finding that the waiver of the stipend in question would eliminate any "material financial effect."

Because none of your questions implicate the provisions of the Political Reform Act, and they all are hypothetical in nature, we likewise decline to answer the specific questions posed in your letter.

_______________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-05-012
Page No. 2

For your information, we have enclosed the pamphlet "Can I Vote," a guide to
preventing conflicts of interest for public officials. If you have any other questions
regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: William J. Lenkeit
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures

I:\AdviceLtrs W-105-012
